growth_records.cite: Sheng-yang Xu et al. Rumen and hindgut microbiome regulate average daily gain of preweaning Holstein heifer calves in different ways. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01844-7. [PMC11264748]
growth_records.snippet: The A. fermentans P41 was cultured in PYG liquid medium for 48 h, followed by acid production detection and untargeted metabolomic analysis. The concentration of volatile fatty acids (VFAs) was measured using gas chromatography... Subsequen
growth_records.notes: Only genuinely experimental prokaryote pure-culture condition in this batch. A. fermentans P41 grown in PYG liquid medium 48 h; VFA measured by GC confirming acetate + butyrate secretion (products qualitatively identified; numeric VFA concentrations reside in Supplementary Fig. S17, NOT in the accessible fulltext, so rate=null, no derivation possible). Medium composition of PYG not given numerically (peptone + yeast extract + glucose only). Culture temperature for the PYG liquid step is not explicitly stated in text; the anaerobic workstation used for isolation/colony purification was held at 37 C, and the separate mixed-community in-vitro rumen fermentation (Menke medium + rumen fluid + substrate) ran at 39 C for 48 h. NOTE: the paper's 'growth rate' language refers to calf average daily gain (host animal), NOT microbial specific growth rate; no mu, no OD, no doubling time for the microbe. The in-vitro fermentation that reported increased butyrate proportion (P<0.05) with P41 addition is a MIXED rumen-fluid community assay reporting molar proportions, not a per-strain rate, so it is not recorded as a separate quantitative record. Other three papers in batch reported NO experimental prokaryote rates: PMC11260450 (RhlE RNA helicase, P. aeruginosa; LB, 37 C, qualitative cold-growth phenotype only); PMC11265412 (Lactobacillaceae PanGEM; all uptake/secretion fluxes FBA/FVA-PREDICTED; the sole experimental glucose uptake used as a lower bound is cited from Elliman-Duplat et al. / L. reuteri JCM 1112 reconstruction, ref 11 = doi:10.1186/s12934-019-1229-3, with no numeric value in main text); PMC11271444 (Salmonella Typhimurium; 'flux'=efflux pump, '13C' incidental, 'secretion'=T3SS).
